title: Bitfarms Stock Analysis: Significant Surge and Promising Prospects
text: Bitfarms operates integrated Bitcoin data centers in Canada, United States, Paraguay and Argentina, owning and operating data centers validating transactions on Bitcoin blockchain and selling computational power for cryptocurrency mining in multiple jurisdictions. Company operates as pure-play Bitcoin mining company focusing on large-scale operations powered primarily by renewable hydroelectricity sources, emphasizing low production costs per Bitcoin, providing competitive advantage in industry where energy expenses dominate profitability. Company sources power from underutilized hydro facilities in regions like Quebec, Canada and parts of South America, achieving some of lowest electricity rates in sector. Bitfarms isn't biggest miner but efficiency-focused model helps it punch above weight, emphasizing low-cost power and geographic diversity compared to giants like Marathon Digital or Riot. Recent hashrate improved to 11.1 EH/s and efficiency to 25 w/TH, with plans to reach 21 EH/s adding 220 MW capacity and positioning as largest miner in South America. Company reported $209 million net loss for 2025 despite 72% revenue increase to $229 million with loss stemming from equipment impairment, Bitcoin price declines and infrastructure renovation. Q3 2025 revenue was at $69.25M with gross mining margin dropping to 35%. Company pivoting to High-Performance Computing (HPC)/AI infrastructure rebranding as Keel Infrastructure addressing post-halving economic pressures and AI computing demand offering higher gross margins.
